The classification of Paspalum psammophilum is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. intro: Paspalum psammophilum, also known as the sand grass, is a perennial grass species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, boasting various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Paspalum psammophilum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Paspalum psammophilum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
